Good overlap on hedging in a high-fuel environment, but does not address the NCC ex-fuel growth threshold at higher capacity.
Direct on loyalty/credit card economics and repeat guests’ impact on yield/booking behavior.
Predicted touches air travel impacts on yields, but not specifically North American airfare sensitivity/behavior.

missed_actual_themes: Turning-the-corner booking trajectory versus February and consumer willingness to book beyond Q3 into 2027
Mexico construction status/resumption for Perfect Day Mexico
Threshold for NCC ex-fuel growth at higher capacity (e.g., 2%-3%)
North American airfare impact on booking behavior for NA itineraries
overpredicted_themes: Orderbook/fleet orders and detailed Icon-class ROIC modeling
Industry supply/orderbook and deployment mix specifics (e.g., Legend redeployment)
Technology/AI attribution and pre-cruise digital metrics
Market share/TAM capture and demographics detail
summary: Coverage was solid (8/12, 67%) with strong matches on yield decomposition, Med/Q3 bookings, destination ramp, hedging, and loyalty impacts. Precision was moderate (57/110, 52%) given many overpredictions on capital returns, JV/TUI, and orderbook topics not asked. Misses centered on construction status, NA airfare effects, and specific booking recovery versus February and 2027 NCC ex-fuel thresholds.
